title: Dupatya schlechtendalii
intro: Dupatya schlechtendalii, also known as Sichuan lovage, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, native to China and widely cultivated for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its reported health benefits of reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion. The bioactive constituents of Dupatya schlechtendalii include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Dupatya schlechtendalii is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to make patent medicines.

The classification of Dupatya schlechtendalii is that of a perennial vine, characterized by its twining stems and showy, trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant is part of the genus Dupatya, which comprises a limited number of species found in the Americas.
